item.title: Victoria's treaty leaders have been elected. Here's how the new structure will work
item.description: The new leaders of Victoria's First Peoples' Assembly say they're pushing ahead with promises made under the state's treaty, despite political uncertainty.

item.title: Alex De Minaur destroys racquet in Italian Open exit as Novak Djokovic also out
item.description: Struggling Alex de Minaur suffers another early clay-court tournament exit, this time at the Italian Open in Rome.

item.title: Australia is breaking its carbon bond, but there's a cost
item.description: As Australia weans itself away from coal, aging power stations across the country have been reduced to smoke and rubble. But for communities at the coalface, there are serious and dramatic consequences.

item.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 hopes you're as distracted as you are nostalgic
item.description: Miranda Priestly has lost her fangs in the reboot as weak plot lines make room for superfluous scenes and characters.

item.title: New bug species Larrakia feather-legged assassin discovered in national park near Darwin
item.description: A new type of bug, celebrated for the "really cool" way it assassinates prey, has been named in honour of traditional custodians in the northern Australia region where it was discovered.

item.title: Client abuse and unpaid labour contributing to poor vet wellbeing
item.description: A parliamentary inquiry makes more than 30 recommendations to address increase of poor veterinary mental health outcomes.

item.title: Iga Świątek's coach Francisco Roig tears Achilies in training match
item.description: Francisco Roig will think twice about betting against his new charge Iga Świątek after the veteran coach tore his Achilles tendon in a volley session in Rome.

item.title: If you are not convinced by sardines, this is the dish to try - ABC News
item.description: This simple and nutritious pasta dish is sure to satisfy those who already adore sardines, and it may just convert those who don't.

item.title: Iron Maiden: Burning Ambition documentary a fan-focused look at metal icons
item.description: The Iron Maiden documentary is a 50-year history of heavy metal's flashiest band as told by their biggest fans.

item.title: Cats spoil Magpies' party as concussion floors Moore, Suns down Saints in Darwin
item.description: A huge collision sees Darcy Moore concussed as Geelong takes control against Collingwood at the MCG as Gold Coast downs St Kilda in Darwin with star defender Cal Wilkie injured.

item.title: CSIRO to persist with cutting up to 350 jobs despite extra $387.4 million in federal funding
item.description: Australia's national science agency CSIRO will persist with job cuts to up to 350 roles despite an extra $387.4 million in funding announced from the federal government.

item.title: Four for the weekend: Lena Dunham, Kneecap and a fix-all stew
item.description: What we're watching, reading and cooking this weekend.

item.title: Maroons lose Tom Dearden to injury ahead of 2026 State of Origin series against Blues
item.description: Queensland selectors have been forced to cope with the news one of their best playmakers will miss up to eight weeks with his ankle injury.

item.title: Farrer by-election: What are the issues?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: One Nation scores historic win in Farrer by-election
item.description: One Nation scores an historic win in the Farrer by-election, marking the first time the party has won a Lower House seat in federal parliament.

item.title: Quiz: Think you've got a way with words? Guy Montgomery's here with your Sunday spelling quiz
item.description: Ready to hone your homophones and verbalise your vowels? Let's get spelling!

item.title: Diagnosed as depression, caused by work. Who pays the cost of burnout?
item.description: Burnout is everywhere — in resignation letters, therapy sessions and late-night emails. Yet the debate over what it is reveals a bigger question: Should workplaces bear responsibility for the damage modern work can cause?

item.title: Iran is trying to formalise its control over the Strait of Hormuz, but there are some obvious problems
item.description: Amid a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, the IRGC has launched a maritime authority for the Strait of Hormuz. The Persian Gulf Strait Authority says ships need to email their application for passage. So, we sent them a request.

item.title: Farrer presents One Nation as a genuine electoral threat rather than just a protest
item.description: The result in Farrer has made One Nation's attempts to become the natural home for conservative Australians look less like a protest and more like a genuine electoral threat.

item.title: Ancient white wine variety reducing water bills and instilling hope for struggling Australian industry
item.description: In an Australian first, two South Australian wine grape growers are trialling an ancient variety from Cyprus that is reducing water consumption and reconnecting them to their Mediterranean roots.

item.title: Historic Melbourne mansion Labassa 'not just a building, it's a story'
item.description: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.

item.title: India embraces cheaper weight loss drugs but doctors are worried
item.description: India has become one of the first countries to introduce cheaper, generic brands of weight loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy, but health professionals warn a lack of regulation and oversight is causing problems.

item.title: Hope for endangered Watson's tree frog as tadpoles released into wild
item.description: Scientists hope the successful captive breeding and release of the endangered Watson's tree frog in Victoria's far east will help aid the species' survival, after its habitat was decimated during the Black Summer bushfires.

item.title: How Nigel Farage moved from the political fringe to contender for UK prime minister
item.description: Nigel Farage has his eye on the prime minister's job himself and, if Reform can sustain this level of support at a general election, the keys to Downing Street may fall to him.

item.title: Jay Vine hospitalised after horror Giro d'Italia crash takes down field
item.description: Jay Vine's awful luck continued on stage two of the Giro d'Italia with the Australian taken to hospital following a horror crash on wet roads that saw multiple riders abandon the race.

item.title: Ongoing breakdowns hamper mobile breast screening service in Tasmania, but government says fix on the way
item.description: Angie is almost the age her mum was when she died from breast cancer, so getting a screening is important to her. But access to Tasmania's screening service has been difficult.

item.title: Restored cider mill crushes first apples in more than 100 years
item.description: A recently restored, hand-powered mill that revolutionised cider-making has crushed its first batch of apples in more than 100 years.

item.title: Iran's IRGC threatens US sites as Trump awaits Tehran response — as it happened
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has threatened to target US sites if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media is reporting. It comes as Washington continues to wait for Tehran's response to its latest negotiating position.

item.title: Home destroyed by fire rebuilt as an Earthship made of tyres, bottles and cans
item.description: Dee-Ann Kelly lost her home to fire in 2024, now she's rebuilding an Earthship, made from recycled materials in an effort to make her home more fire resilient.

item.title: Love story: Love when you don't know how long you've got
item.description: My 14-year-old daughter Pip has Williams syndrome. The future is uncertain and full of heartache, but my love for my baby is not.

item.title: Rockhampton sisters raise $70,000 for Leukaemia Foundation in honour of cherished mum
item.description: Elli Wust was pregnant with her first child when she found out her mum had a rare blood cancer. Now she has two children who never got to meet their grandmother, who died at age 58.

item.title: Storm snap record losing run as Tigers coach laments 'negative smell'
item.description: The Melbourne Storm are winners for the first time since round two, snapping a club-record losing streak with a big win over the Wests Tigers.

item.title: Rockliff backs away from revealing reasons for backbencher Shelton legal costs
item.description: Tasmania's premier is under fire for backtracking on an undertaking to explain why taxpayers paid a $15,000 legal bill for a backbencher.

item.title: Injury concern for women's world number one Aryna Sabalenka ahead of French Open
item.description: Women's tennis world number one Aryna Sabalenka has a fortnight to recover from injury before the French Open after her loss in Rome.

item.title: Fuel supplies boosted in South Australia by $40m strategic diesel reserve
item.description: A strategic diesel reserve of between 10 million and 20 million litres will be established in SA to bolster the supply and provide assurance to farmers, the state government says.

item.title: Wellington Phoenix progress to A-League Women's grand final with victory over Brisbane Roar
item.description: Wellington Phoenix has become the first side from New Zealand to make an A-League grand final after inching past Brisbane Roar in their semifinal.

item.title: Split Enz: Tim Finn on the band’s history, legacy and reunion shows
item.description: Tim Finn on the past, the present and the possible future of Split Enz.

item.title: This week in history quiz, May 11: How much did Michael Jordan's signed Air Nike 1s sell for?
item.description: From the Tudor period right through to the 21st century, test your knowledge of the past with this week's history quiz.

item.title: Fashion designers are experimenting with AI, but not everyone is a fan
item.description: As Australian Fashion Week gets underway in Sydney, local and international designers debate the merits and detractions of using AI in the industry.

item.title: When is the 2026–27 federal budget? Here's what you need to know before tomorrow
item.description: Budget night features a lot of political talk and policy wonkiness, but the government's plan will impact your financial situation in one way or another, so it's quite important. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Aged care patients stranded in hospitals prompt calls for government action
item.description: The number of aged care patients stuck in hospitals has jumped by 35 per cent in less than a year, fuelling fresh demands for the federal government to stem the crisis.

item.title: Military officer's arrest over alleged embassy break-in becomes lightning rod in Japan-China tensions
item.description: An incident involving a Japanese soldier allegedly scaling the wall of China's embassy in Tokyo a month ago has become a lightning rod for tensions between China and Japan.

item.title: Inside the course targeting young tradies with get-rich schemes
item.description: "Escape the 9-5" content is blowing up on social media, but people who have gotten caught up in it warn of the costs.

item.title: AI recruited to speed up home approvals, in budget set to reshape housing market
item.description: Artificial intelligence will be recruited to speed up assessments of housing and energy projects waiting for environmental approval, as the government races to build more homes and ward off a key frustration turning voters away from the major parties.

item.title: Anzac Day Bledisloe Cup Test between Wallabies and All Blacks can be a major win
item.description: Australian rugby lacks a marquee event that captures the national imagination each year, but this could change if a bold Bledisloe Cup proposal gets approval.

item.title: Antarctic ship Nuyina's refuelling saga unresolved as deadline looms, documents show
item.description: A costly refuelling saga affecting Australia's Antarctic icebreaker might not be resolved for another three years under most options, internal government documents reveal.

item.title: Authorities evacuate passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Netanyahu says Iran's uranium must be 'taken out' before war ends— as it happened
item.description: The Israeli prime minister says Iran's stockpile of enriched uranium must be "taken out" before the war against Iran can be considered over.

item.title: Paul Magnier wins stage three of Giro d'Italia 2026, Jay Vine 'lucky' to avoid serious injury in stage two crash
item.description: Jay Vine's wife Bre takes to social media to express thanks for everyone's support following her husband's sickening fall at the Giro d'Italia.

item.title: Mary Fowler's Manchester City beats Sam Kerr's Chelsea in FA Cup semifinal, Dario Vidošić's Brighton beats Liverpool
item.description: Mary Fowler's goal kick-started Manchester City's FA Cup comeback to down Sam Kerr's Chelsea and set up a final against Brighton, led by former Socceroo Dario Vidošić.

item.title: Arsenal beats West Ham thanks to VAR call with massive implications at top and bottom of EPL ladder
item.description: Another late goal and a controversial VAR call have helped Arsenal stay in control of the Premier League title race, while delivering another blow to relegation-threatened West Ham United.

item.title: Two mums on having babies in their 40s
item.description: Whether it's about overcoming health challenges, waiting to meet "the right person" or wanting to be financially stable, Australian women are increasingly having children later in life.

item.title: Ambulance Victoria condemns assault on pregnant paramedic in Melbourne
item.description: A pregnant paramedic has been assaulted in Melbourne less than a fortnight after the stabbing of a colleague, the union says.

item.title: Traffic camera captures 'spectacular' meteor breaking up over Tasmania
item.description: Tasmanians across the state reported seeing the bright lights of a meteor last night. Astronomer Martin George says the spectacle was typical of a natural object burning up in the atmosphere.

item.title: Victoria to fund free meningococcal B vaccines for Year 10 students
item.description: The government will fund a $9 million program to vaccinate students against meningococcal B from January 1 next year.

item.title: Western Australia will fail to achieve net-zero by 2050 on current trajectory, Woodside-funded report warns
item.description: Woodside-commissioned modelling suggests WA is not on track to reach net zero by 2050, even if its massive Browse gas project proceeds, unless renewable energy is rolled out at unprecedented speed.

item.title: Police investigate reports of hundreds of pregnant sheep stolen from Great Southern farm
item.description: A farmer in Western Australia's Great Southern region has lost about $100,000 after someone stole hundreds of his pregnant sheep.

item.title: Queensland's beleaguered DNA lab clears backlog of hundreds of sexual assault kits, director says
item.description: Forensic Science Queensland was the subject of two commissions of inquiry, over failures in testing and cultural issues which may have led to miscarriages of justice.

item.title: Hitchhiking northern quoll released back into wild in central Queensland
item.description: Members of the public spotted the marsupial fleeing the wheel arch of a truck in Brisbane and alerted authorities.

item.title: First Nations man dies in custody at Lotus Glen Correctional Centre in Far North Queensland
item.description: The death is not being treated as suspicious and a report is being prepared for the coroner. Fourteen Indigenous people have died in Australian prisons this year.

item.title: Hasrat Gill stumbled upon the T20 World Cup on TV one day. It changed her life forever
item.description: State and WBBL cricketer Hasrat Gill has risen through the ranks of youth Australian cricket. She is one of many South Asian teenagers flocking to the sport.

item.title: Cafe-worthy breakfast and brunch recipes for relaxed mornings
item.description: From shakshuka to French toast, you can enjoy an indulgent breakfast or brunch at home with one of these recipes.

item.title: Aboriginal man expresses remorse after avoiding jail for vandalising culturally sensitive site
item.description: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.

item.title: Canberra to Sydney rail link gets $100 million upgrade to improve train speeds
item.description: The federal, ACT and NSW governments will spend $100 million upgrading the Canberra to Sydney rail link in a bid to reduce the train trip to under four hours.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship passengers to quarantine in Perth
item.description: Australian passengers who were on board the hantavirus-hit cruise ship will be ordered to quarantine in Perth for at least three weeks.

item.title: Josh Rachele urges fans to 'choose kindness' after receiving abusive message online
item.description: Adelaide Crows footballer Josh Rachele has called out fan behaviour after receiving an abusive message online amid an ongoing conversation on mental health in the AFL.

item.title: ICC confirms arrest warrant for Philippine Senator Ronald Dela Rosa over war on drugs
item.description: Video footage displayed during the Senate session showed Philippine senator Ronald Dela Rosa running away from Philippine agents as the International Criminal Court confirmed an arrest warrant had been issued.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Hadley's shock return 10 weeks post-baby
item.description: Paige Hadley made a shock return to the Super Netball league less than three months after giving birth to her baby boy. Despite some commentary online questioning her priorities, the midcourter is more determined than ever to prove women can pursue multiple dreams.

item.title: US, Iran reach stalemate in war negotiations dashing hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he's aiming for "complete victory" in Iran as the US and Iran reach an impasse in negotiations.

item.title: How Mortal Kombat II brought Hollywood to the Gold Coast
item.description: The $110 million Mortal Kombat sequel is the next in a laundry list of productions putting Queensland on the radar for international films.

item.title: The caring burden for single, childless women
item.description: Single women over 45 without children are much more likely to be spending time caring for family members who are aging, have a disability or chronic illness. And it's not just costing them financially.

item.title: Cricket Australia hopes Big Bash League privatisation could make it one of sport's 'grand slams'
item.description: Cricket Australia chief executive Todd Greenberg says private investment in the Big Bash League is "inevitable" and waiting for too long could mean "we've missed the boat" as more competitions pop up around the world.

item.title: Trump and Xi's Beijing summit must confront the AI cold war
item.description: When Trump last visited China in 2017, artificial intelligence was not yet the centre of global power. Now it is.

item.title: Homes Tasmania might come and go before Felicity gets a rental
item.description: Felicity Hirst says she is not too picky about what kind of house she lives in; she just wants one. She has been on Tasmania's social housing list for three and a half years — as long as the state's housing authority has existed.

item.title: Study finds link between air pollution exposure and risk of ending up in hospital
item.description: A new study has found a clear link between air pollution exposure and the risk of ending up in hospital. It's a battle Julia Ovens knows only too well.

item.title: Efforts to secure buyer for Liberty Bell Bay smelter ongoing after window for offers closes
item.description: The window for non-binding offers to purchase for the Liberty Bell Bay smelter, in Tasmania's north, has officially closed, with a union delegation taking their cause to Canberra as workers anxiously await news.

item.title: Queensland man pioneers app to fly drones using head movements
item.description: Ever since he was a young child, Christopher Hills has wanted to fly an aircraft. Now he has made that dream a reality.

item.title: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to develop over Australia, delivering a broad band of rain
item.description: In a welcome twist of weather variability, the first north-west cloudband of 2026 will finally develop over Australia this week, delivering a broad band of rain.

item.title: Legal Aid NT to consider more cuts amid funding shortfall, months after services slashed
item.description: One of two legal bodies responsible for representing vulnerable Territorians in court is considering making further cuts, six months after its services were previously slashed.

item.title: Small robots used to help students consider careers in renewable energy
item.description: Students are using tiny, programmable robots to code in a bid to encourage them to consider careers in renewable energy due to predicted skill shortages.

item.title: Alexei Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Jannik Sinner at Italian Open
item.description: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

item.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
item.description: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.

item.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
item.description: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

item.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts
item.description: Discussing how people want to be treated at the end of their life can be hard, but experts say planning can bring dignity and comfort.

item.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
item.description: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

item.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved
item.description: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

item.title: Captain of Hantavirus-hit cruise ship praises crew and guests
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship's captain praises crew and guests
item.description: The MV Hondius's captain has praised the patience and kindness of his crew and guests as they disembark.

item.title: Jessica Pegula putting family experience in NFL and NHL to use in tennis prize money protest
item.description: Jessica Pegula is putting her family experience of the NFL and NHL to use in tennis players' fight against the French Open for increased prize money.

item.title: Sitting near the Strait of Hormuz, seafarers are in limbo as Iran war drags on
item.description: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.

item.title: Australian government finalising plans to bring hantavirus cruise ship passengers home
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of the deadly hantavirus outbreak are "in good health", as the Australian government remains hopeful it will finalise plans to repatriate them within 48 hours.

item.title: What giving up alcohol taught me about myself
item.description: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

item.title: New laws to deter organised criminals recruiting young people introduced to NSW parliament
item.description: New offences and stronger punishments for the use of so-called kill cars and the recruitment of children by organised criminals have been introduced in the Parliament of NSW.

item.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'
item.description: Indian communities across New Zealand say they are feeling "hounded" after a surge in racist incidents.

item.title: Four ministers quit as UK PM Starmer tells cabinet he is staying put
item.description: The British prime minister has defied calls to resign, telling ministers he will "get on with governing".

item.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi
item.description: A multiple sclerosis patient says he waited three hours for an access cab, only to be told in the middle of the night one would not be available, forcing him to spend the night at an Adelaide emergency room.

item.title: Bungled Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement project has cost $717 million more than first forecast
item.description: The Spirit of Tasmania ferry replacement program has cost $717 million more than was forecast in the original business case, a parliamentary inquiry has heard.

item.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access
item.description: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

item.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
item.description: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

item.title: Tax reform to help 75,000 buyers into first homes, government says ahead of federal budget's release
item.description: In a political push to get ahead of criticism it has broken election promises, Labor is making a case for how wealth is taxed in Australia by promising the budget will open the housing market to younger buyers.

item.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
item.description: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

item.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
item.description: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

item.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

item.title: Emotional Senate tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby amid debate on focus of NT inquiry
item.description: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

item.title: Federal budget 2026: Winners and losers
item.description: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors. So who wins and who misses out?

item.title: Government breaks promise with restrictions to negative gearing and capital gains tax discount in federal budget
item.description: Tax perks that property investors have used for decades to maximise profits and offset their losses will be restricted from tonight, in one of the most significant changes to the tax system in years.

item.title: A five-minute guide to what's in the federal budget to help with cost of living
item.description: Fresh tax cuts, an instant $1,000 tax reduction and cheaper medicines: here's what the federal government's latest budget means for Australians.

item.title: With this federal budget, Australia's last baby boomer PM becomes King of the Kids
item.description: Tonight, Anthony Albanese becomes the first serving prime minister in decades to take a hammer to the most sacred shibboleth in Australian domestic politics.

item.title: Why Chalmers reined in the property tax lurks in federal budget 2026
item.description: One of the more striking elements from the now well-entrenched program of pre-budget leaks is the lack of outrage from the population or even the opposition.

item.title: $250 tax break for millions of workers in federal budget 2026
item.description: Labor will raise taxes on investment properties and some trust funds, giving the revenue back to workers with a new $250 tax break unveiled in the federal budget.

item.title: Labor unveils 'ambitious' plan for tax reset in 2026 budget
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Government says economic environment demands bold reform
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor bets the house on young Australians' desperation to buy their own
item.description: If the reforms help younger Australians believe home ownership is within reach, Labor can expect to collect a political dividend.

item.title: Jayson Gillham on legal battle with Melbourne Symphony Orchestra and his tour Keys to Life
item.description: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

item.title: Blues On Broadbeach celebrates 25 years of music by the sea
item.description: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

item.title: Will CGT and negative gearing budget changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
item.description: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

item.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget
item.description: The ABC has asked Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, about the latest federal budget.

item.title: Kaden Groves abandons Giro d'Italia 2026 on stage four, Jhonatan Narváez claims win for battered UAE Team Emirates
item.description: Jhonatan Narváez dedicated his Giro d'Italia stage four victory to his fallen UAE Team Emirates colleagues as another Aussie fell by the wayside as the race hit Italian soil.

item.title: Big utes remain popular for Australians in March and April despite rising fuel costs
item.description: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.

item.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species
item.description: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.

item.title: Victorian emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition
item.description: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.

item.title: Australian-born, London-based MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to oust Britsh PM Keir Starmer
item.description: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

item.title: Construction underway on seniors public housing in Sydney's south-west, despite local opposition
item.description: New public housing units built specifically for seniors are under construction in Sydney's south-west, despite opposition from some residents.

item.title: High Court hears bid to overturn block on 22-year Mount Pleasant coal mine extension
item.description: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.

item.title: Trump labels critical news reports on Iran 'virtual treason' - as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week.

item.title: Federal budget disappointment for farmers and environmental groups
item.description: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

item.title: Jannik Sinner equals record with Novak Djokovic for consecutive Masters 1000 match wins
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner extended his Masters 1000 winning streak to 31 matches, equalling Novak Djokovic's all-time record.

item.title: PM defends tax overhaul as Coalition accuses him of 'lying' — as it happened
item.description: The Coalition accuses the prime minister of "lying" about changes to tax settings as Anthony Albanese defends the overhaul as necessary for younger Australians.

item.title: Commonwealth Bank shares slump 10.4pc in biggest one-day fall on record
item.description: It was the worst day on the stock market for Australia's biggest bank since March 2020, when it dropped about 10 per cent, with sentiment also weighed by a trading update and broader weakness in bank stocks.

item.title: $1.5b Trump Tower on Gold Coast scrapped less than three months after deal struck
item.description: The branded tower proposed for Surfers Paradise would have been Australia's tallest building.

item.title: Former Carlton AFL coach Michael Voss says club in better place since he took over
item.description: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

item.title: Trump calls reporter 'stupid' after being questioned about economic policies
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Georgia rugby players, including former captain, banned over drug test scheme
item.description: Former Georgia captain Merab Sharikadze was one of six players handed lengthy bans from rugby for their part in a scheme involving alleged sample substitution and advance warnings of drug tests.

item.title: Iran war boosts Beijing ahead of Trump-Xi summit in China, experts say
item.description: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.

item.title: Sunrisers Hyderabad bowled out for 86 in IPL loss to Gujarat Titans
item.description: Australian Test cricketers Travis Head and Pat Cummins have been part of a heavy loss in the Indian Premier League.

item.title: MV Hondius passengers quarantine, fight disease as Dutch and UK governments order isolations
item.description: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

item.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?
item.description: It's been 18 years of hosting Gruen for Wil Anderson, but the self-confessed advertising cynic says the topic is more relevant than ever.

item.title: Tax cut calculator: How much could the 2026 federal budget measures save you?
item.description: How much the budget measures could benefit you depends on how much you earn.

item.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
item.description: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

item.title: Political fight brewing over budget tax reform as Coalition vows to fight Labor's changes
item.description: The Coalition vows to do all it can to prevent property tax reform, which the shadow treasurer says will increase rents and make it harder for young people to buy a house.

item.title: Goldfields women's shelter forced to adapt as abusers use tracking technology
item.description: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

item.title: First openly gay NBA player Jason Collins dies at 47 of brain tumour
item.description: Collins, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died from an aggressive brain tumour, his family announced Tuesday.

item.title: Australia announces squad for Women's T20 Cricket World Cup, Darcie Brown left out
item.description: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

item.title: Paratroopers jump to remote community to deliver medical support
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Inquest into 2023 tasering death of 95-year-old Clare Nowland begins
item.description: Three years after the great-grandmother was tasered by a police officer in her aged care home, an inquest has heard from police and ambulance officers who were at the scene.

item.title: Eurovision semi finals kick off with musical nod to Austria-Australia confusion
item.description: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

item.title: Five-minute solar eclipse in WA's north to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere'
item.description: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

item.title: Gippsland man charged with threatening federal politician on social media
item.description: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

item.title: Community mourns as Mornington Peninsula's Sammy the seal believed dead
item.description: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

item.title: Government watches as Ravenswood Gold mine sets refinancing deadline
item.description: Queensland's largest gold mine has set a refinancing deadline, despite high global gold prices.

item.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
item.description: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

item.title: Adam Scott brings good form into 99th consecutive golf major at PGA Championship
item.description: Adam Scott is being lauded for his "amazing" longevity as he prepares to tee up for a 99th consecutive major golf championship.

item.title: Here's how federal budget negative gearing and capital gains tax changes will affect you
item.description: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

item.title: Hamas 'systematically and deliberately' used rape on October 7, 2023, Israeli not-for-profit group says
item.description: A 300-page report from an Israeli non-profit organisation has detailed allegations of horrific sexual assault against victims of Hamas's October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel, accusing the group of "systematically and deliberately" using rape and sexual torture.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

item.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

item.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist urged to test for bloodborne viruses
item.description: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

item.title: Peak Jewish body says $600 million federal budget response to antisemitic Bondi terror attack 'modest'
item.description: The Executive Council of Australian Jewry has welcomed a $600 million federal budget funding commitment for the Jewish community, but says it is a "small comfort" for those who lost loved ones in the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Man, 52, charged with defrauding Commonwealth after police seize suspected military ordnance
item.description: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

item.title: High Court sides with TasPorts over 2022 Mersey River tugboats sinking by Goliath
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

item.title: Proposed new laws to protect WA home buyers if builders fold in wake of Nicheliving's collapse
item.description: New legislation introduced to state parliament aim to prevent Western Australians from having to suffer the financial and emotional strain of not having a home if their building company collapses.

item.title: CFMEU inquiry hears union members allegedly planted asbestos at Cairns construction site
item.description: The inquiry heard the CFMEU was making social media posts attacking the credibility of WHSQ employees.

item.title: Ex-Bikie boss David Pye, who paid sniper to assassinate rival, sentenced to 34 years in prison
item.description: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

item.title: Free omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA for pregnant women in a bid to prevent preterm birth
item.description: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

item.title: Premier David Crisafulli and ministers Amanda Camm and Tim Mander referred to CCC over relationship saga
item.description: The call for investigation follows weeks of scrutiny over whether ministers Amanda Camm and Tim Mander had made appropriate conflict of interest declarations relating to their relationship.

item.title: Police investigate after woman's body found in Joondalup CBD
item.description: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man assisting with inquiries.

item.title: Bendigo's Hargreaves Mall could reopen to cars under plan to revitalise area
item.description: A Victorian council is proposing to reopen a regional mall to vehicles after 44 years in an attempt to breathe new life into the area.

item.title: Steven May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt retirement on eve of AFL season
item.description: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

item.title: Four charts show Labor's budget is not just about the generation gap, but the wealth gap too
item.description: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.

item.title: Yindjibarndi urged to consider appeal against native title compensation decision
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

item.title: Blast furnace worries grow as Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' to federal budget
item.description: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

item.title: John Travolta's luxury plane bound for Shellharbour museum arrives by boat
item.description: After nearly a decade of delays and millions of dollars in repairs, John Travolta's old luxury jet needed a boat to get to Australia.

item.title: Court grants mining giant Bravus leave to sue council for $22 million over unsealed access road
item.description: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

item.title: US woman Kouri Richins, who published a book on grief after husband's death, to be sentenced for his murder
item.description: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave the Panthers is as unprecedented as their success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.

item.title: Labor upgrades net migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
item.description: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.

item.title: Veterans warn Invictus Australia budget cut could be life-threatening
item.description: The federal government is urged to reverse its decision to cut funding to Invictus Australia, an organisation that supports veterans.

item.title: Lawyer for notorious paedophile Peter Liddy says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'
item.description: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

item.title: NSW Premier Chris Minns responds after committee report dubs grants scheme a 'slush fund'
item.description: Chris Minns responds to claims from a parliamentary inquiry that the government "unlawfully redirected" millions of dollars of public money and accused witnesses of lying under oath.

item.title: More than 2,000 protesters chant 'stop the chop' to oppose North Adelaide golf project
item.description: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

item.title: Queensland banking on 'unbelievable' Tamika Upton to level State of Origin series
item.description: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

item.title: NRL's Magic Round to remain in Brisbane until 2032 after government reaches deal for contract extension
item.description: Queensland's deal with the NRL to host Magic Round was originally due to end after 2027, but the state government has reached a deal with the NRL to extend until 2032.

item.title: Proposed changes to NT child protection laws spark fears of 'generations' of harm
item.description: Peak Aboriginal bodies have accused the Northern Territory government of using Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged murder as political cover to push ahead with sweeping changes to child protection laws.

item.title: What's in the 2026 budget for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Cannon by Lee Lai becomes first graphic novel to win the Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

item.title: Australia secures aircraft to bring passengers home from Hantavirus-hit cruise ship
item.description: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the Hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.

item.title: Gunshots ring out at Philippine senate as ICC seeks arrest of former police chief
item.description: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.

item.title: Donald Trump and Elon Musk arrive in Beijing ahead of Xi Jinping summit
item.description: The ​US and China are expected to agree to forums to facilitate mutual trade and investment.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage, breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift which he did not declare.

item.title: Israeli strikes on vehicles near Lebanon capital Beirut kill at least 12
item.description: The deadly drone strikes come on the eve of a new round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon in the United States.

item.title: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy accuses Russia of attempting to overload Ukraine's defences in an hours-long onslaught.

item.title: Princess Kate tours Italy in first overseas visit since cancer recovery
item.description: The royal is visiting Reggio Emilia, the Italian city famed for its approach to early childhood education.

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
item.description: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

item.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
item.description: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

item.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
item.description: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

item.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
item.description: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

item.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': The rising populist tide is making Labor bolder
item.description: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

item.title: Igor Arrieta wins wild Giro d'Italia stage, despite crashing and taking a wrong turn
item.description: The Giro d'Italia treated supporters to one of the most astonishing finishes to a bike race in history when Igor Arrieta had to twice regather himself after first crashing and later taking a wrong turn to claim a stunning victory.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in China but only one has the upper hand
item.description: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.
